prompt: Generate an image: View looking straight up from the base of a massive ancient tree into its canopy. The trunk at the bottom center connects to a few large branches. Each of those branches splits into more branches. Each of those splits into yet more. The branching multiplies outward at every level — every point in the structure connects to fewer things below it and vastly more things above it. The canopy fills the entire sky, an overwhelming explosion of connections. The perspective makes the viewer feel small and singular at the base, surrounded by an endlessly more connected world above. Late afternoon golden light filtering through. Photorealistic, cinematic, slightly vertiginous, awe-inspiring and unsettling simultaneously. No people, no text, no labels, no overlays of any kind.
visual_logic: A tree viewed from below: the trunk (you) has few connections; every branch above connects to more branches than the one below it — every node sees a more-connected world above it, always, as a structural inevitability.
